skill.name: d3-viz
skill.description: Create interactive D3.js charts, graphs, network diagrams, geographic views, and custom SVG data visualizations.
skill.summary: Build interactive D3.js visualizations with chart patterns, interactivity, transitions, scales, colors, and accessibility guidance.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static shell execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and the D3.js name in documentation. The only retained concern is a D3 CDN example at SKILL.md line 36, which is a legitimate external dependency reference but not evidence of malware or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External CDN Reference in Setup Example
low_findings.description: The setup section includes a hardcoded d3js.org CDN URL. This is a common documentation pattern, but users who copy it will load code from an external host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly present in a D3 setup example. It is an external dependency risk, but no credential access, tracking, or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches occur in Markdown code fences, JavaScript template literals, and inline Markdown code. No executable Ruby, shell command, or command runner is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visible Markdown or JavaScript examples in SKILL.md. The skill only contains documentation and does not define scripts or shell execution tooling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography finding points to the description line mentioning D3.js. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is frontmatter text describing D3.js visualization. There is no DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic use on that line.

content.value_statement: Custom data visualization often needs more control than standard chart libraries provide. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through D3.js patterns for interactive SVG charts.
actual_capabilities: Explains when D3.js is a good fit for custom visualizations.
Provides setup guidance using npm imports or a D3 CDN script.
Shows a reusable workflow for SVG dimensions, margins, scales, axes, and data binding.
Covers bar charts, line charts, pie charts, tooltips, zoom, pan, and transitions.
Lists common D3 scale types and color scheme options.
Recommends validation, responsive behavior, performance checks, accessibility labels, and cleanup.
limitations: It is a compact reference, not a full D3.js course.
It does not include complete project scaffolding or test setup.
It does not provide advanced geographic projection details.
It recommends Canvas for large datasets but does not explain Canvas implementation.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Custom Chart
use_cases.description: Create a D3 chart structure with margins, scales, axes, and bound SVG elements.

use_cases.title: Add Interactive Exploration
use_cases.description: Add tooltips, zoom, pan, and transitions to make a visualization easier to inspect.

use_cases.title: Choose the Right Visualization Tool
use_cases.description: Decide when D3.js is better than Chart.js, Recharts, or Three.js for a task.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Chart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple D3 chart.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to create a basic SVG chart for this dataset. Include margins, scales, axes, and data binding.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Chart Type
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to adapt an existing visualization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to convert this visualization into a D3 bar, line, or pie chart. Explain the scale and mark choices.

prompt_templates.title: Add Interaction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to add tooltips, zoom, pan, and transitions to this D3 visualization. Keep the interaction accessible.

prompt_templates.title: Review Visualization Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for maintainability and performance review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to review this D3 visualization for data validation, cleanup, responsiveness, performance, and accessibility.

output_examples.input: A request for a custom scatter plot with numeric x and y values.
output_examples.output: A structured D3 plan that defines dimensions, margins, linear scales, axes, circles, and a cleanup step before redraw.

output_examples.input: A request to make an existing chart easier to explore.
output: Zoom and pan behavior for dense data.
Accessibility notes for labels and readable values.

output_examples.input: A request to choose between D3.js, Chart.js, Recharts, and Three.js.
output_examples.output: A recommendation based on chart complexity, interaction needs, 3D requirements, and whether a standard chart library is sufficient.

best_practices: Validate data for null, missing, and invalid numeric values before rendering.
Use responsive sizing and redraw logic when the container changes.
Clean up previous SVG elements before rendering updated data.
anti_patterns: Do not use D3.js when a simple chart library meets the requirements.
Do not render thousands of SVG elements without checking performance.
Do not omit accessibility labels for interactive or published charts.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create D3.js charts, graphs, network diagrams, geographic views, and custom SVG visualizations.

faq.question: Does it support interactivity?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for tooltips, zoom, pan, and animated transitions.

faq.question: Can it help choose between chart libraries?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use D3.js and when to consider Chart.js, Recharts, or Three.js.

faq.question: Does it include complete application code?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and snippets that an assistant can adapt inside an existing project.

faq.question: Is there any security concern?
faq.answer: The only retained concern is a CDN setup example. Users can avoid it by installing D3 through npm.

faq.question: Which AI coding tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
